items.situation: student asking where to find practice exams
items.speaker: Where can I find last year's practice exams?

options.A: Have you checked the professor's course page?
options.B: The exam mostly covers the first five chapters.
options.C: I finished my history essay last night.
options.D: Yes, the format is multiple choice.

items.explanation: 说话人问“在哪里能找到去年的模拟卷”。A 用反问引导对方去课程主页，正好把地点信息推进给对方，是自然的口语应答，因此正确。B 只谈考试内容范围，答非所问（关键词 exam 陷阱）。C 说自己写完了历史作业，与话题完全无关。D 回答的是考试题型，而不是“在哪里找”，答错了问题类型。

items.situation: checking why a service is closed
items.speaker: Why is the post office closed today?

options.A: I mailed my package there yesterday.
options.B: Oh, it's a national holiday today.
options.C: The line there is always too long.
options.D: She works at the bank downtown.

items.explanation: 说话人问“邮局今天为什么关门”。B 直接给出原因——今天是国定假日，是对 why 问题的正确回答。A 说昨天在那里寄过包裹，是借用 post office 联想的关键词陷阱，没解释原因。C 抱怨排队太长，答非所问。D 说她在市中心银行上班，与话题无关。

items.situation: calling a shop to check if it is still open
items.speaker: I wasn't sure if you're still open tonight.

options.A: The jar was really hard to open.
options.B: Thanks, I had a great weekend.
options.C: We'll stay open until nine for you.
options.D: No, I've never been there before.

items.explanation: 说话人其实想知道店铺今晚是否还营业。C 没有简单回答“开着”，而是直接解决其真正需求——告诉对方营业到九点，属于 context_shift，因此正确。A 里的 open 指打开罐子，是一词多义陷阱。B 谈周末过得愉快，答非所问。D 否认自己去过，误解了说话人的意图。

items.situation: two classmates discussing a group project deadline
items.speaker: We're really cutting it close on this deadline.

options.A: Please close the window; it's cold.
options.B: The cafeteria ran out of coffee again.
options.C: Yes, I locked the classroom door.
options.D: Let's buckle down and finish it.

items.explanation: 说话人感叹快赶不上截止时间了。D 用习惯表达 buckle down（沉下心来）回应，提议一起加把劲完成，最自然，因此正确。A 里的 close 指关窗，是一词多义陷阱。B 说食堂又没咖啡了，与话题无关。C 说锁了教室门，误解并答非所问。

items.situation: student checking how often the shuttle runs
items.speaker: How often does the campus shuttle come?

options.B: I left my bag on the shuttle.
options.C: It stops right outside the library.
options.D: The parking lot is completely full today.

items.explanation: 说话人问“校车多久来一趟”。A 直接给出频率——大约每十五分钟一趟，正确回答了 how often。B 说把包落在了车上，是借用 shuttle 的关键词陷阱。C 回答的是停靠地点，而不是间隔时间，答错了问题类型。D 说停车场满了，与话题无关。

items.situation: responding to an invitation despite a schedule conflict
items.speaker: Are you free to meet up this Friday?

options.A: Yes, the tickets were free yesterday.
options.B: Sorry, my plate is full then.
options.C: The restaurant serves breakfast until noon.

items.explanation: 说话人邀请对方周五见面。B 用习惯表达 my plate is full（我那时忙不过来）委婉拒绝，是自然应答，因此正确。A 里的 free 指票免费，是一词多义陷阱。C 说餐厅供应早餐到中午，答非所问。D 回答的是地点，答错了问题类型。

items.situation: friend inviting another to a study group
items.speaker: Do you want to join our study group tonight?

options.A: The group project is due next week.
options.B: The weather looks nice this evening.
options.C: I'll bring my notes and snacks.
options.D: No, I don't have their number.

items.explanation: 说话人邀请对方今晚参加学习小组。C 没有简单答“好”，而是承诺带笔记和零食一起来，实际推进了对话，属于 context_shift，因此正确。A 谈小组作业下周才交，是借用 group 的关键词陷阱。B 说今晚天气不错，答非所问。D 说没有他们的电话，误解了邀请的意思。

items.situation: classmate asking what was missed in lecture
items.speaker: What did I miss in today's lecture?

options.A: The lecture hall was freezing cold.
options.B: My laptop battery died at lunch.

items.explanation: 说话人问“今天的课我错过了什么”。D 以话语标记 Actually 开头，间接说明自己也没去，因此帮不上忙——是自然且贴切的应答，正确。A 说教室很冷，是借用 lecture 的关键词陷阱。B 说笔记本电脑没电了，答非所问。C 回答的是下课时间，答错了问题类型。

items.speaker: My car won't start again this morning.

options.A: Want me to give you a ride?
options.B: I just washed my car yesterday.
options.C: The bakery on Main Street closed.
options.D: No, I already returned the keys.

items.explanation: 说话人抱怨车又打不着火了。A 用反问主动提出捎对方一程，直接解决其困境，是自然应答，因此正确。B 说昨天刚洗过车，是借用 car 的关键词陷阱。C 说主街的面包店关门了，与话题无关。D 说已经还了钥匙，误解了对方的意思。

items.situation: student seeking reassurance about a presentation
items.speaker: Do you think my slides look okay?

options.A: The slide broke at the playground.
options.B: Honestly, they look really polished to me.
options.C: I need to buy more coffee later.
options.D: Yes, the projector is in room five.

items.explanation: 说话人想确认幻灯片做得好不好。B 以话语标记 Honestly 开头给出肯定评价，是自然、贴切的应答，因此正确。A 里的 slide 指滑梯，是一词多义陷阱。C 说自己要去买咖啡，答非所问。D 谈投影仪在几号教室，答错了问题类型。
